175900: What is Disaster Energy Assistance Disaster Energy Assistance is a temporary emergency program that helps eligible households and families afford energy heating services after a natural disaster or an emergency. The program offers help for eligible low-income households to offer, restore or continue heating sources. Disaster Energy Assistance is available until funds are exhausted. Who is eligible To be eligible for Disaster Energy Assistance, a household must have at least 1 US citizen or eligible non-citizen and: Have income equal to or less than 60% of the state median income Have resided in 1 of the identified 25 disaster declared counties listed during Hurricane Helene and are currently residing in 1 of those 25 counties Have been impacted and suffered a loss because of Hurricane Helene What does the program cover Utility bills, reconnection costs/fees, deposits, and meter repair Propane/fuel oil tanks Natural gas/propane line repair Propane/fuel oil delivery Insulation replacement Necessary wiring/circuit board replacement to be able to heat home Repair or replacement costs for furnaces. Coats and blankets, as tangible benefits to keep individuals warm. Space heaters/electric heaters. Individuals in 1 of the identified 25 disaster declared counties can apply in-person or via telephone with their l (https://www.ncdhhs.gov/localDSS)ocal county Department of Social Services. 184300: Regulates investor-owned agencies such as Duke Energy, Dominion Energy, PSNC Energy, and more. Helps customers make payment arrangements, offers information, and answers questions about billings. Investigates complaints by customers of the public utility companies. Rights of Utility Consumers: NOTE: The following rules only apply to utility companies that are regulated by the NC Utilities Commission - they do not apply to towns and membership cooperatives that provide utilities. Someone must be given a specific number of days to pay their bill. For example, electric and gas bills include a past-due date that is at least 25 days after the billing date. If the bill has not been paid by the past-due date, the company can charge a late fee of up to 1%. If payment is late, the utility company must provide a written notice at least 10 days before they disconnect service. The notice generally states that the amount that must be paid within 10 days to keep service from getting cut off. The company must make contact again at least 24 hours before they disconnect service. From November 1 through March 31, utility companies cannot disconnect service to households that: include someone who is disabled or age 65 or older; are unable to pay under an installment plan; and are certified by a local social services agency as eligible to receive assistance under an energy assistance program. Power cannot be disconnected on Fridays, weekends, state or federal holidays, or the day before a state or federal holiday. What companies are regulated by the NC Utilities Commission The following are examples of companies regulated by the NC Utilities Commission. The Commission does not regulate companies such as electric membership cooperatives or towns/municipalities. Duke Energy Progress Duke Energy Carolinas Dominion Energy/PSNC Energy Frontier Natural Gas Piedmont Natural Gas For more information, visit ncuc.gov/Consumer/whatwedo.html (https://www.ncuc.gov/Consumer/whatwedo.html). How does someone apply for the utility disconnection protection From November 1 through March 31, utility companies cannot disconnect service to households that meet the following requirements: have someone who is disabled or age 65 or older (or both) are unable to pay under an installment plan are certified by a local social services agency as eligible to receive assistance under an energy assistance program Duke Energy: People must get a "Certification Utilities Moratorium" form from the Department of Social Services. The form must be mailed to Duke Energy, 9700 David Taylor Drive, Charlotte, NC 28262. duke-energy.com/home/billing/special-assistance/moratorium (https://www.duke-energy.com/home/billing/special-assistance/moratorium) Dominion Energy/PSNC Energy No specific instructions. People should call 866-366-4357 for electric or 877-776-2427 for gas. Frontier Natural Gas: No specific instructions. People should call 336-526-2690. Piedmont Natural Gas: No specific instructions. People should call 800-752-7504
